description: Sandbox Glob Pattern Matching - Convert glob patterns to seatbelt regex filters for OS-level sandbox deny/allowlist rules

userStories.title: Glob to regex core conversion module
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a utility that converts glob patterns to seatbelt-compatible POSIX ERE regex strings so that the sandbox profile generator can use (regex ...) filters for glob patterns.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/sandbox/glob-to-regex.ts with exported globToSbplRegex(pattern: string, projectRoot: string): string function
Create exported isGlobPattern(pattern: string): boolean that detects *, ?, [, { wildcards
* converts to [^/]* (does NOT cross /), ** converts to .* (crosses /), ? converts to [^/]
Strict standard glob semantics: * does not cross /, must use ** for cross-directory matching
Relative patterns resolved against projectRoot to produce absolute-path regex

Fixed prefix realpath resolution: split by /, stop at first segment containing wildcards, realpath the prefix, append remaining glob segments. Example: src/lib*/*.key -> realpath(src) + /lib[^/]*/[^/]*\.key
Special regex characters (. ( ) + ^ $ | \ ] ) are properly escaped before conversion
Output regex is anchored with ^ and $ for full-path matching
Output is valid POSIX ERE (no \d, \w etc. - use character classes)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: deniedOperations filtering for sandbox deny rules
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the sandbox init to respect deniedOperations from security rules, so that write-only deny rules don't generate sandbox deny entries (since sandbox deny blocks both read and write).
acceptanceCriteria: Modify src/sandbox/init.ts deny pattern extraction to check deniedOperations
Only pass rule to sandbox deny when deniedOperations includes 'read' OR includes 'llm' (llm equals read+write)
When deniedOperations is only ['write'] (no 'read', no 'llm'), do NOT generate sandbox deny for that rule
Pass deniedOperations info through SandboxConfig instead of just pattern strings - update the deny field type in SandboxConfig interface (src/sandbox/index.ts)
Update profile.ts resolveDenyEntry to accept the new deny entry format
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Profile generator glob detection and dispatch
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the profile generator to detect glob patterns and automatically choose between literal/subpath (concrete paths) and regex (glob patterns), so that the generated seatbelt policy correctly handles both cases.
acceptanceCriteria: Modify src/sandbox/profile.ts resolveDenyEntry: when isGlobPattern returns true, call globToSbplRegex to generate (deny file-read* file-write* (regex "...")) rule
Modify src/sandbox/profile.ts resolveAllowlistEntry: when isGlobPattern returns true, call globToSbplRegex to generate (allow file-write* (regex "...")) rule
When isGlobPattern returns false, keep existing literal/subpath behavior unchanged
Glob patterns ignore type: directory|file field - regex matches full path, does not distinguish files from directories
Deny regex rules placed AFTER allowlist regex rules in profile output to ensure deny wins (seatbelt last-match-wins)
Add comment annotation before regex rules showing original glob: ;; glob: *secretchat* followed by the regex rule
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: SandboxConfig interface and seatbelt.ts glob compatibility
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the SandboxConfig and Sandbox interfaces to support platform-agnostic glob conversion so that Linux Landlock can later implement its own glob handling.
acceptanceCriteria: SandboxConfig in src/sandbox/index.ts preserves original pattern strings in allowlist and deny entries (no glob preprocessing at interface level)
Glob detection and conversion logic stays in profile.ts (profile generation layer), not in SeatbeltSandbox class, so different platforms can implement their own conversion
Fix seatbelt.ts generatePolicy isDir detection: patterns containing wildcards must not use !pattern.includes('.') heuristic - pass through original type or skip type inference for glob patterns
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Covered by US-002 (SandboxConfig + seatbelt.ts changes) and US-003 (glob logic in profile.ts)

userStories.title: Unit tests for glob to regex conversion
userStories.description: As a developer, I need comprehensive unit tests for the glob-to-regex conversion to ensure correctness across all supported patterns.
acceptanceCriteria: Create test/sandbox/glob-to-regex.test.ts
Test *secretchat* -> matches only project root files with secretchat in name (does not cross /)
Test **/*secretchat* -> matches files at any depth with secretchat in name
Test **/*.key -> matches .key files at any depth
Test src/**/*.test.ts -> matches .test.ts files under src/ at any depth
Test *.swift -> matches .swift files in project root only
Test src/Crypto*.swift -> matches Crypto-prefixed .swift files in src/
Test src/lib*/*.key -> fixed prefix is src, matches src/lib-xxx/foo.key
Test concrete paths (no wildcards) -> isGlobPattern returns false
Test special character escaping: . ( ) + are not treated as regex metacharacters
Test projectRoot joining and fixed-prefix realpath resolution
All tests pass via bun run test:parallel --pattern '**/test/sandbox/glob-to-regex.test.ts'
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Integration tests for sandbox profile with glob rules
userStories.description: As a developer, I need integration tests verifying that glob patterns in deny/allowlist produce correct seatbelt profiles end-to-end.
acceptanceCriteria: Create test/sandbox/profile-glob.test.ts
Test generateProfile with deny glob pattern -> outputs (deny ... (regex "...")) rule
Test generateProfile with allowlist glob pattern -> outputs (allow ... (regex "...")) rule
Test mixed concrete + glob patterns -> concrete uses literal/subpath, glob uses regex
Test deny regex appears AFTER allowlist regex in output (verify last-match-wins ordering)
Test deniedOperations filtering: ['read','write'] -> generates sandbox deny, ['llm'] -> generates sandbox deny, ['write'] only -> does NOT generate sandbox deny
Test seatbelt.ts generatePolicy end-to-end with glob patterns
All tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: sandbox status displays regex rules with glob annotations
userStories.description: As a user, I want sandbox status to clearly show which rules use regex (from glob) versus literal/subpath, so I can verify my glob patterns are correctly converted.
acceptanceCriteria: lark-opencode sandbox status SBPL Profile output shows regex rules with comment annotation of original glob pattern
Format: ;; glob: **/*secretchat* on line before (deny file-read* file-write* (regex "..."))
Existing literal/subpath rules display unchanged
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Covered by US-003 profile.ts changes - glob annotations already in SBPL output
